text: The harbor town of Vellmar sits where two grey rivers braid into the sea, its houses leaning inward as though sharing a secret too heavy to hold alone. In autumn the fog arrives like a slow tenant, settling into doorways and softening the edges of the bell tower until it seems half-remembered. The cobbles keep the rain long after the clouds have gone. There is a bakery that opens before dawn, and its warmth, spilling into the dark street, feels less like comfort than like a small, stubborn argument against the cold pressing patiently at every window.
